---
title: "L929 Cell Propagation Medium"
subtitle: "Abdullah Lab, IMMEI, University Hospital Bonn"
protocol_id: "BUF-L929-PROP"
version: "v1.0"
date: "2026-03-31"
description: "This medium is used for routine propagation of L929 cells for LCMV production workflows."
---

**Protocol ID:** {{< meta protocol_id >}}
**Version:** {{< meta version >}}
**Author:** Dillon Corvino

# Purpose

This medium is used for routine propagation of L929 fibroblasts. L929 cells are used as an alternative host cell line for LCMV propagation, particularly for certain strains such as Traub.

\tableofcontents

# Linked protocols

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)

this_buffer <- rmarkdown::metadata$protocol_id
deps <- read_csv(here::here("protocols_src/_dependencies.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

linked <- deps |>
  filter(buffer_id == this_buffer) |>
  distinct(protocol_id)

if (nrow(linked) == 0) {
  cat("None\n")
} else {
  linked |>
    mutate(line = glue::glue("- `{protocol_id}`")) |>
    pull(line) |>
    cat(sep = "\n")
}
```

---

# Components (Final volume: 500 mL)

| Component                                      | Amount    | Final concentration |
|------------------------------------------------|-----------|---------------------|
| DMEM (high glucose, with L-glutamine)          | 475 mL    | Base medium         |
| Heat-inactivated FCS/FBS                       | 25 mL     | 5% (v/v)            |

# Preparation

1. In a sterile biosafety cabinet, add ~450 mL of DMEM to a sterile 500 mL bottle.
2. Add 25 mL heat-inactivated FCS/FBS.
3. Top up with DMEM to a final volume of **500 mL**.
4. Mix gently by inversion.
5. Label with:
   - "L929 propagation medium (DMEM + 5% FCS)"
   - Date of preparation
   - Initials
6. Store at **4°C** for up to **4 weeks**.

# Use

- Warm medium to **37°C** before use with cells.
- Discard medium if there is any sign of contamination (turbidity, threads, pH shift) or if it is past the defined expiry (4 weeks from preparation).

# Notes

- L929 cells are mouse fibroblasts commonly used for LCMV propagation.
- The lower FCS concentration (5%) compared to other cell lines is standard for L929 maintenance.

# Version history

| Version | Date       | Author         | Changes                                |
|---------|------------|----------------|----------------------------------------|
| v1.0    | 2026-03-31 | Dillon Corvino | Initial Quarto buffer document.        |
